Q1 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The SFD and BMD for a beam are shown in the figures below. Corresponding loading diagram would be:

 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The SFD does not change, while the sudden change in BMD denotes a concentrated moment at point C.
The triangular shape of BMD shows concentrated load is applied at free end.

Q3 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A bar of length 6.0 m is made up of a structural steel having stress-strain diagram as shown in figure. The yield stress of steel is 250 MPa and slope of initial linear part of stress-strain curve is 200 GPa. The bar is loaded axially until it elongates 8.5 mm and then load is removed. The
elongation of the bar after removing the load is _____ mm. (Round off to three decimal places).

 
NAT
Correct answer: 1.0002
Solution

1.0002 (1.0000 - 1.0003)

NAT

Solution

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

NAT
Correct answer: 2
Solution

2 (2 - 2)

NAT
Correct answer: 5
Solution

5 (5 - 5)

Q31 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Match List-I (Properties) with List-II (Definition) and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:

 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Toughness - Area under load-deflection curve.
Resilience - Area under the elastic part of the load-deflection curve.
Creep - Time dependent permanent deformation under constant load.
Hardness - Resistance against scratching.

NAT

Solution
